primary_field: agent discipline and the 9 BRIDLE rules

observation: Scribe BRIDLE opened. Primary field covers the 9-rule preamble Founder-approved B113; every Knight prompt carries it. Witnesses prompt-engineering (Level 2) shared with Scribe R9, and verify-before-assert (Level 3) shared with the SP-21 Tidbit Scribe ledger.

BRIDLE Rule 2 (verify before asserting) + serial-dispatch pattern validated empirically: K433 Knight caught a K432 bug (PedestalStakeAdmin querying public.pedestal_holders instead of upekrithen()) while performing session-hygiene reads of K431/K432 work. Pattern: each subsequent Knight reads prior Knight's output as EVIDENCE, not trust — so bugs surface at the next session boundary rather than at launch. Worth a sentence in THE BRIDLE commentary and in any ops-discipline doc. The bug caught would have been invisible at K432 launch because K432 tests were unit-level, not integration-level against the actual admin routes.
